heroSection.title_h1: We are the new era of investing with Economic Justice | ATTLAS

solutionsSection.title_h1: SOLUTIONS AND BENEFITS DESIGNED FOR YOU
solutionsSection.description_p: At the ATTLAS Foundation, we work for the dignity and well-being of the most vulnerable: seniors, children, homeless people, and animals. Our mission is to provide comprehensive support that transforms lives and communities. We reorient economic justice toward projects that promote equity and social development. Our programs seek to empower communities through sustainable and supportive initiatives.

feature1.title: Animal Shelter and Care
feature1.description: We support the rescue, rehabilitation, and adoption of homeless animals, providing them with love, veterinary care, and a safe environment.

feature2.title: Solidarity Food
feature2.description: We strengthen initiatives that provide nutritious meals to older adults and children in vulnerable situations, ensuring their physical and emotional well-being.

feature3.title: Home for Older Adults
feature3.description: We support shelter initiatives for older adults without family support, guaranteeing a dignified and supportive old age.

feature4.title: Conciliation Center
feature4.description: Conciliation center for financial insolvency and debt negotiation.

solutionsSection.onu_desa:
The Attlas Org Foundation, REGISTERED in the database of the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UN DESA - ONU), promotes the project "Colombia Debt Relief: A New Beginning", an initiative supported by the law firm Propensiones Internacional.

This program represents an innovative model of economic justice, designed to strengthen asset recovery and financial stability for vulnerable sectors through advanced legal mechanisms and specialized financial strategies. Based on the principles of international cooperation and sustainable development, this project not only seeks to alleviate the economic burdens of thousands of families, but also to establish a legal framework that guarantees their social inclusion and long-term stability.

csr.title: Economic Justice with a Human Heart
csr.p1: Economic Justice is not a theory: it is the hope we transform into action. In Quindío, we believe that every senior citizen deserves a hot meal, every child a childhood filled with nutrition and love, and every stray animal a dignified shelter and care. Here we don't talk about numbers; we talk about dignity, life, and opportunity.
csr.p2: Inspired by the principles of the solidarity economy—which prioritizes equity, cooperation, and the common good over individual profit—we commit before God to building real, deeply human economic justice. We believe that:
csr.p3: Solidarity is the best currency: when we share resources, we generate trust and build support networks that sustain our communities.
csr.p4: Redistribution is not about subtracting: it is about adding life by offering access to food, shelter, and care to those who need it most. Why Is economic justice vital in Quindío?
csr.p5: In our department, where many families live on the edge of uncertainty, economic justice bridges the gap between fragility and strength. Through food for seniors and children, we open doors to health and stability. By providing loving shelters, we restore the dignity of those who have been forgotten: the elderly and our most vulnerable pets.
csr.p6: Because when we feed bodies, we comfort souls; when we protect life, we sow hope; and when we empower communities, we build a more just, supportive, and humane Quindío.

gallery.alt2: Delivery of school kits to low-income children
gallery.alt3: Support and companionship for the elderly in nursing homes

card.description1: Protecting the environment is not only preserving nature, it is guaranteeing the dignity and well-being of the communities in the Department of Quindío that depend on it.
card.description2: At the Attlas Foundation, we work on projects that unite sustainability, human development, and international cooperation, because we believe that every local action can transform our global future.
card.description3: Today, humanity needs strong alliances to defend life in all its forms: our own, that of ecosystems, and that of future generations.

whyUsSection.title_h1: Humanitarian Innovation with Economic Justice for the
whyUsSection.description_p: At the Attlas Foundation, we develop humanitarian projects in the Department of Quindío, focusing on our lines of support for community development, values ​​education, and family well-being. We want to transform local realities and generate a global impact from the green heart of Colombia.

heroSection.description_p: We transform every challenge into an opportunity, offering fair, equitable, and sustainable solutions with a social approach and in defense of fundamental and human rights. Because stability and dignity are everyone’s right.

requirementsSection.description_p: We offer multiple benefits and guarantees for individuals and companies facing financial difficulties.

feature1.title: Who does it apply to?
feature1.description:
• Natural Persons
• Merchants
• Workers and Employers
��� Families
• Property Owners and Tenants
• Public and Private Entities
• Debtors and Creditors
• Victims of Rights Violations
• Community and Social Organizations
• People in a Situation of Insolvency

feature2.title: Requirements
feature2.description:
• Valid identification
• Formal conciliation request
• Legitimate interest in the conflict
• Matter subject to conciliation according to law
• Willingness of the parties to conciliate
• Payment of fees (if applicable)
• Documentary support of the case
• Legal representation (if applicable)
• Updated contact information
• Authorization for data processing

feature3.title: Main Guarantees
feature3.description:
• Impartiality and Neutrality
• Confidentiality of the Process
• Access to Justice
• Respect for Fundamental Rights
• Legality and Legal Certainty
• Voluntariness of the Parties
• Efficiency and Procedural Speed
• Equity in the Agreements
• Recognition of Human Rights
• Transparency in Management

feature4.title: Budgets
feature4.description:
• Quick and effective conflict resolution
• Reduced legal costs
• Confidentiality and information protection
• Equitable access to justice
• Avoid prolonged legal proceedings
• Encourages dialogue and cooperation
• Legal certainty in reached agreements
• Protection of fundamental and human rights
• Flexibility in dispute resolution
• Restoration of relationships between the parties

step1.title: Start Your Insolvency Process
step1.subTitle: Economic Insolvency for Natural Persons
step1.advisor: The collection and use of data in this form are to comply with the following <u>Legal Requirements</u>:

1.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025.
1.description: Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012, “Article 539. Requirements for the debt negotiation request. The debt negotiation request <bold>must be filed directly by the debtor</bold>, who may appear in the proceeding accompanied or represented by legal counsel. <bold>Their attendance with or through legal counsel shall be mandatory when the minimum amount is exceeded.</bold>”
1.example: I, ____________, of legal age, identified with Citizen ID No. ____________ of ____________, am a natural person ___ merchant, based on the provisions of Title IV of Law 1564 of 2012, especially Article 531 et seq., as amended by Law 2445 of February 11, 2025, as well as its regulatory development contained in Decree 2677 of 2012, through this document I appear before your office for the purpose of REQUESTING DIRECTLY THE FORMAL OPENING OF THE DEBT NEGOTIATION PROCESS, under the terms established by current regulations, due to cessation of payments and the constitutional principles enshrined in articles 1, 13, 25, 29, 53 and 332 of the Political Constitution of Colombia and the articles of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR, 1948), I appoint as legal counsel the licensed attorney, Dr. JORGE URIEL VEGA LONDOÑO, of legal age, identified with Citizen ID No. ____________ of ____________, practicing attorney, holder of Professional Card No. ____________ of the Honorable Superior Council of the Judiciary, with registered email ____________.

2.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 9 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 8 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
2.description: “Article 538. <bold>Insolvency assumptions</bold>. For the purposes set forth in this title, it shall be understood that the natural person <bold>may avail themselves of insolvency proceedings</bold> when they are in a state of cessation of payments.”

3.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
3.description: “Article 539 #1 A <bold>report</bold> that indicates in a <bold>precise manner the causes that led to the cessation of payments</bold>.”
3.accompaniment: To draft the following text, get support from one of our legal advisors, who will help you identify the causes of your insolvency and draft the text appropriately.

4.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
4.description: “Article 539 #2 The <bold>proposal</bold> for <bold>debt negotiation</bold>, which must be clear, express and objective.”
4.accompaniment: To draft the following text, get support from one of our legal advisors, who will help you identify the causes of your insolvency and draft the text appropriately.
4.example:
Based on the principles of good faith, transparency, actual payment capacity and progressivity in economic recovery, the undersigned proposes to his creditors the following payment plan, which reflects his voluntary and honest willingness to fulfill his obligations according to his current economic situation and reasonable improvement projections:

FIRST: It is proposed to deliver assets in payment in kind that may help reduce the outstanding balance, considering their commercial value and applicable legal provisions to safeguard the rights of all parties.

SECOND: The offsetting of retained or deposited funds in favor of creditors is proposed, in order to partially or fully settle the respective obligations, in accordance with current regulations and guaranteeing equity among the different creditors.

THIRD: A monthly recovery installment is established, calculated based on the debtor's current payment capacity, which shall be distributed according to legal priority and the guidelines agreed upon in the insolvency process.

FOURTH: Once the agreed reorganization period ends, the debtor shall continue paying obligations with longer terms, maintaining their commitment to comply with the agreements and adjusting installments as their economic situation evolves favorably.

FIFTH: When there are solidarity funds or special contributions withheld by creditor entities, it is requested that such resources be applied as a direct payment or offset to the debt, avoiding duplicate withholdings and contributing to the strengthening of the liability pool.

Note 1: Creditors are requested to consider a partial waiver of interest, both current and default, as a sign of good faith and to facilitate effective compliance with the payment plan.

Note 2: This proposal is made under the principle of transparency and willingness to pay. The debtor states his intention to increase contributions as his economic situation improves, in order to promote an integral and equitable solution.

Qualification regarding the sacrifice of essential assets: It is recognized that this plan implies a significant effort by the debtor, who in some cases might be forced to reduce expenses intended for basic needs. Nevertheless, the commitment to prioritize his obligations without renouncing fundamental rights or the minimum subsistence is maintained.

5.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
5.description: “Article 539 #3: A <bold>complete and updated list</bold> of <bold>all creditors</bold>, in the <bold>order of credit priority</bold> set forth in articles 2488 et seq. of the Civil Code.”

6.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
6.description: “Article 539 #4 A <bold>complete and detailed list</bold> of his/her <bold>assets</bold>, if any, including those held abroad. Estimated values and data necessary for their identification must be indicated, as well as detailed information on <bold>liens, encumbrances and precautionary measures</bold> affecting them, and it must be indicated which of them are <bold>subject to family housing and which are unseizable family assets</bold>.”

7.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
7.description: “Article 539 #5 A <bold>list</bold> of <bold>judicial proceedings</bold> and any administrative or private proceedings of a patrimonial nature carried out by the debtor or pending against him, <bold>indicating the court</bold> or the <bold>office where they are filed</bold> and <bold>their current status</bold>”

8.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
8.description: “Article 539 #6 <bold>Certification of the debtor's income</bold> issued by his employer or pension fund or, if self-employed, a declaration thereof”

9.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
9.description: “Article 539 #7 Amount of <bold>available resources for payment of obligations after deducting necessary expenses for the subsistence of the debtor and dependents</bold> if any, expenses for asset preservation and those of the proceeding.”

10.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
10.description: “Article 539 #8 Information regarding whether or not there is a <bold>current marital or patrimonial partnership</bold>.”

11.title: COMPLIANCE WITH ARTICLE No. 10 OF LAW 2445 OF FEBRUARY 11, 2025. Which amended ARTICLE 539 OF LAW 1564 OF 2012.
11.description: “Article 539 #9 A breakdown of alimony obligations in charge, indicating amount and beneficiaries and attaching the certificate from the Registry of Delinquent Alimony Debtors REDAM”

step1.accept_terms: I acknowledge that I have read and understood the legal requirements to start the economic insolvency process.
step1.accept_terms_and_conditions: I accept the <termsLink>terms and conditions</termsLink> and the <privacyLink>personal data processing policy</privacyLink>.

step2.title: Personal Information
step2.subTitle: Please complete the following information
step2.example: I, <firstName>{firstName}</firstName> <lastName>{lastName}</lastName>, of legal age, identified with Citizen ID No. <documentNumber>{documentNumber}</documentNumber> of <expeditionCity>{expeditionCity}</expeditionCity>, am a natural person <isMerchant>{isMerchant}</isMerchant> merchant, based on the provisions of Title IV of Law 1564 of 2012, especially Article 531 et seq., as amended by Law 2445 of February 11, 2025, as well as its regulatory development contained in Decree 2677 of 2012, through this document I appear before your office for the purpose of REQUESTING DIRECTLY THE FORMAL OPENING OF THE DEBT NEGOTIATION PROCESS. For contact, my phone is <cellPhone>{cellPhone}</cellPhone> and my email is <email>{email}</email>.

step3.title: Declaration of Cessation of Payments
step3.subTitle: Please read the following declaration
step3.declarationLabel: I voluntarily accept that I comply with the declaration of cessation of payments.
step3.declarationText: I declare, under oath, that I hold the status of a natural person NON merchant and that I am in a state of cessation of payments, complying with the regulatory requirements set forth in Article 539 of Law 1564 of 2012 amended by Law 2445 of February 11, 2025, since I have two (2) or more overdue obligations for a period exceeding ninety (90) days, and the amount of my unpaid obligations represents no less than thirty percent (30%) of the total liabilities in my charge. Consequently, the opening of the debt negotiation proceeding is appropriate, in accordance with Title IV of the General Procedure Code, guaranteeing compliance with the principles of good faith, balance, transparency and preservation of the minimum subsistence, which govern the insolvency regime for the natural person NON merchant.
step3.declarationNote: If you do not comply with this declaration, please contact one of our legal advisors to help you determine if it is possible to start the insolvency process.

step4.title: Cessation of Payments Report
step4.subTitle: Please complete the following information

form.terminationReport: Detail here the causes that led you to the cessation of payments situation
form.error: Please complete the cessation of payments report
form.useAI: Use AI to improve the cessation of payments report

step4.exampleTitle: Examples of cessation of payments report
step4.example1: During the period between March and June 2023, the termination of my employment contract with company XYZ, together with a drastic reduction in hours and the closure of key projects, led to the loss of stable income. In addition, the crisis resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic worsened my situation, putting both personal and family financial stability at risk. Therefore, I request the opening of the insolvency proceeding to restructure my debts and protect the minimum vital assets, allowing me to recover economic stability and guarantee the well-being of my family nucleus.
step4.example2: Since 2022 I have been taking care of two elderly adults with chronic medical conditions, which has created a complete economic dependency on my income. High costs for medications, therapies and special care have absorbed most of my resources, leading me to default on financial obligations. I request the opening of the insolvency proceeding to achieve a viable restructuring that guarantees family well-being.
step4.example3: In August 2023 I experienced a marital breakup that involved separation from the household and assuming expenses that were previously shared, including rent, utilities and my children's education. This situation caused a severe imbalance in my personal finances, affecting my ability to meet previously acquired credit obligations. Therefore, I request to be covered by the natural person insolvency regime.
step4.example4: During the second half of 2023, the educational institution where I worked reduced my working hours by 50% due to a decrease in school enrollment. This generated a drastic drop in my income, preventing me from covering financial obligations such as educational loans, credit cards and maintenance expenses. Additionally, the increase in the cost of living deepened my economic imbalance. For these reasons, I formally request the opening of the insolvency proceeding to protect my minimum vital assets and stabilize my finances.
step4.example5: Since November 2022 I faced a diagnosis of a serious illness that affected my ability to work, forcing me to assume expensive medical treatments not fully covered by the EPS. At the same time, automatic deductions for wage garnishments exceeded 50% of my net salary, compromising my resources for basic needs. Due to this situation of over-indebtedness and financial inability, I request the opening of the insolvency proceeding to restructure my obligations and guarantee my well-being and that of my family.
step4.legalCauses:
• Loss of employment or substantial decrease in income
  • Serious illnesses, costly medical treatments or disability
  • Economic dependency of family members or dependents
  • Excessive indebtedness from consumer credit, wage garnishments or credit cards
  • Fortuitous events or force majeure (natural disasters, theft, pandemics)
  • Failed businesses or asset losses
  • Garnishments, account freezes or disproportionate wage deduction orders
  • Death of a household contributor
  • Prolonged unemployment of the debtor or spouse
  • Severe alteration in the country's economy or the sector where he/she works
  • Loss or reduction of the service contract
  • Disproportionate increase in the cost of living (inflation, utilities, rent)
  • Errors in the structuring of granted credits (induced over-indebtedness)
  • Separation or marital breakup that affected household economy
  • Simultaneous payment of mortgage installments and other marital obligations
  • Financial burden due to children's studies or education
  • Contractual breaches by third parties that affected the debtor's cash flow

exclusion.label: Exclude the asset
exclusion.legalReferencesDescription: The minimum vital assets are those necessary for the subsistence of the debtor and his family, and cannot be seized or subject to precautionary measures. This includes assets such as the family home, furniture and fixtures necessary for the household, and other assets that guarantee basic sustenance.
exclusion.legalReferences:
• Art. 1, 42, 44 and 51 C.P.C.
  • Art. 25.1 Universal Declaration of Human Rights
  • Art. 11 International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights
  • Ruling t-785 of 2006
  • Rulings: t-583/13, t-585/06, t-239/13, t-043/17, SU-111/18, C-599/10, C-747/12, C-555/21
